title: eremalche parryi subsp. kernensis
intro: Eremalche parryi subsp. kernensis, also known as Kern Indian cotton or Kern eremalche, is a rare, endemic subspecies of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is a shrub-like perennial with showy, pink to purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine by Native Americans, particularly the Ohlone people, for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide health benefits such as wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves are used in herbal preparations, often made into teas, infusions, or salves, and may also be used in modern herbal remedies under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
